title: impatiens hochstetteri subsp. jacquesii
intro: Impatiens hochstetteri subsp. jacquesii, also known as the Jacques' touch-me-not, is a species of flowering plant in the balsam family Balsamaceae. It is primarily native to Madagascar and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. The plant's leaves and stems contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of various health issues, including digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The leaves can be prepared as a tea or infused in oil to make herbal preparations for topical application or oral consumption.
